Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1 Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze an event log against a normative process description I need to identify which cases deviate from the intended process flow and explain the violations based on the provided text

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2 Analyze the Normative Process Description Start Always begins with Receive order Next Step Check inventory must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment Sufficient Inventory Path Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Insufficient Inventory Path Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Key Constraints Rules Authorize payment only after Check inventory and before Package items Package items only after Authorize payment and before Ship order Ship order only after Package items and never after Cancel order Close order must be the final activity in all scenarios Deviations like shipping after cancellation authorizing payment before checking inventory or packaging before authorization are violations Ambiguities Interpretation The text mentions room for interpretation but strongly emphasizes the constraints I should stick to the explicit rules but note if something is borderline The rules are actually quite clear

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3 Analyze the Event Log Case by Case Case 1 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Check against rules Matches the Sufficient Inventory Path exactly All constraints met Verdict Compliant Case 2 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Check against rules Package items occurs before Authorize payment The text explicitly says Package items should only occur after authorization packaging items before authorization would be considered a violation Verdict Violation Packaging before payment authorization Case 3 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Check against rules After Cancel order it proceeds to Ship order The text explicitly says Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Also mixing authorization payment with cancellation is illogical per the description cancellation path should go directly to Close after Cancel Shipping after cancellation is a direct violation Verdict Violation Shipping after cancellation Also authorizing payment before cancellation is contradictory to the described paths but the explicit never after a cancellation rule for shipping is the clearest violation Case 4 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Check against rules Matches the Insufficient Inventory Path exactly Verdict Compliant Case 5 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Check against rules Skips Package items The text says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping since items must be prepared before they can be shipped Skipping packaging violates the required sequence Also Ship order should only occur after packaging Verdict Violation Skipping Package items Shipping without packaging

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 4 Synthesize Findings Structure Response List compliant cases briefly 1 and 4 for context but focus on violations as requested For each violating case 2 3 5 state the case ID the observed sequence the specific rule violated and quote reference the normative description Address ambiguities if any though the rules are fairly explicit I can note how I interpreted the skip in Case 5 or the mixed path in Case 3 Ensure tone is analytical and directly answers the prompt Structure Introduction Case-by-case analysis focusing on violations Summary of violations Explanation referencing the text Conclusion

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check against constraints Case 2 Violates Package items should only occur after authorization Case 3 Violates Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Also violates the branching logic cancel path shouldnt have authorization or shipping Case 5 Violates Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping and the explicit sequence requirement Skipping a mandatory step is a deviation All align perfectly with the prompts rules Check for completeness The prompt asks to identify which cases deviate and explain violations referencing the text Ive covered 2 3 5 Ill explicitly state 1 and 4 are compliant Ill structure it clearly Ill also add a note on interpreting ambiguities The prompt mentions potential ambiguities but provides strong explicit constraints I interpreted the constraints strictly because the text uses definitive language must occur should only occur never after would be considered a violation The skipped step in Case 5 is treated as a violation because the text frames packaging as a necessary prerequisite for shipping

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self-Correction Verification during thought Case 3 also has Authorize payment before Cancel order Is that a violation The text says if inventory is insufficient it should directly proceed to Cancel order So authorizing payment before canceling is also a deviation from the described branch logic Ill mention it as a secondary violation context Case 5 skips packaging The text says Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order So skipping it is definitely a violation All good I will write out the response clearly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Structure of response Overview Detailed analysis per violating case 2 3 5 Reference to normative rules Brief note on compliant cases 1 4 Conclusion Summary Matches prompt requirements exactly Output matches response Proceed Self-Correction Refinement during drafting I should make sure I explicitly address the ambiguity part of the prompt The prompt says However the exact nature... I will note that while the prompt acknowledges potential ambiguity the explicit constraints provided are definitive enough to classify these specific deviations as clear violations Ill frame the interpretation carefully All set
